data.title: Mining companies are shifting from Bitcoin mining to AI, leading to a rise in the stock prices of Keel and Hive.
data.description: Shares of Keel Infrastructure and Hive Digital Technologies rose on April 22. Keel completed the sale of its Paso Pe mining farm in Paraguay, raising $13 million to completely exit the Bitcoin mining business and shift its focus to high-performance computing and AI development in North America. Hive Digital Technologies completed a $115 million private funding round, which will be used to purchase GPUs and develop data centers. Shares of the two companies have risen by more than 40% and 31%, respectively, in the past month. In addition, Core Scientific announced plans to issue $3.3 billion in high-yield bonds to fund its transformation into AI infrastructure.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Keel and Hive Shares Rise Amid AI Expansion Plans
data.description: Shares of Keel Infrastructure and Hive Digital Technologies saw significant gains on Wednesday, with Keel rising approximately 9% and Hive over 7%. According to NS3.AI, both companies are advancing their artificial intelligence expansion strategies. Keel Infrastructure announced the completion of the sale of its Paso Pe mining site in Paraguay for $13 million. Meanwhile, Hive Digital Technologies successfully closed a $115 million private offering of convertible notes aimed at purchasing GPUs and developing data centers. These moves indicate a strategic shift away from Bitcoin mining, as Bitcoin's price hovered around $79,000.

data.title: US Republican Senator: US May Take Military Action Against Iran if Peace Agreement Fails to Be Reached
data.description: U.S. Republican Senator Roger Marshall stated that the United States might intervene militarily in Iran if a peace agreement cannot be reached quickly. He believes that previous U.S. presidents have faced similar choices, pointing out that internal divisions within Iran, coupled with U.S. pressure and sanctions, are weakening Tehran's position. Marshall added that he believes in President Trump's strategy and stated that the goal remains to achieve peace as soon as possible. (Jinshi)

data.title: SpaceX warns investors that its orbital AI data center may not be commercially viable.
data.description: In its IPO filing, SpaceX stated that orbital AI computing and its industrialization efforts on the Moon and Mars are still in their early stages, involving significant technological complexities that may prevent commercialization. Elon Musk has previously stated that space-based AI computing is the obvious choice, believing that orbit could become the lowest-cost location for AI within two to three years. The filing warns that orbital AI data centers operate in the harsh and unpredictable space environment, and systems are prone to failure. Furthermore, delays in Starship development may also limit the company's growth strategy. SpaceX currently targets a valuation of approximately $1.75 trillion and plans to raise $75 billion in the coming months.

data.title: U.S. Bank Groups Request Extension on Stablecoin Rule Proposals
data.description: U.S. bank trade groups have urged the Treasury Department and the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) to extend the comment periods for three rule proposals under the GENIUS Act concerning stablecoin oversight. According to NS3.AI, the groups emphasized that these proposals are contingent upon the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency's (OCC) final framework. They have requested a minimum of 60 days for comments following the conclusion of the OCC's rulemaking process. The GENIUS Act is scheduled to be implemented by 2027.

data.title: Ronin's Migration to Ethereum Layer-2 Set for May 12
data.description: Ronin is scheduled to transition to Ethereum layer-2 on May 12, marking a significant change after four years as a sidechain. According to NS3.AI, this migration will reduce RON inflation from over 20% to less than 1%. Ronin developers have announced that the shift to the OP Stack will necessitate approximately 10 hours of mainnet downtime, occurring between 11 a.m. and 9 p.m. ET. Additionally, the migration will redirect 90 million RON from staking allocations to the Ronin treasury.

data.title: U.S. Banking Groups Criticize Rapid Stablecoin Regulation Moves
data.description: U.S. banking groups have expressed concerns that several federal agencies are advancing stablecoin regulations too swiftly, according to CoinDesk. This rapid pace, they argue, complicates the understanding of how these new rules will interact with existing regulations. The banking groups are urging for a more coordinated approach to ensure clarity and consistency in the regulatory framework governing stablecoins.

data.title: Ethereum's L2 Strategy Faces Design Flaw Challenges
data.description: Ethereum's Layer 2 (L2) strategy is encountering significant challenges due to a fundamental design flaw, according to CoinDesk. Alexis Sirkia highlights these issues in the latest Crypto Long & Short Newsletter, emphasizing the need for improvements. Additionally, Jennifer Rosenthal discusses the importance of safeguarding those who are actively building decentralized finance (DeFi) infrastructure. These insights underscore ongoing debates within the crypto community about the future and sustainability of Ethereum's scaling solutions.

data.title: Structural Issues Make Bridges Vulnerable
data.description: Bridges in the crypto ecosystem face inherent vulnerabilities due to their reliance on complex systems with shared infrastructure and hidden trust assumptions, according to CoinDesk. These structural problems suggest that as long as these dependencies exist, bridges will continue to be susceptible to potential threats.

data.title: Ethereum's Staking Ratio Hits Record as Bear Flag Signals Potential Drop
data.description: The ETH/BTC trading pair is currently forming a bear flag pattern, indicating a potential 10% decline towards 0.026 BTC in May. According to NS3.AI, this technical setup suggests a bearish outlook for Ethereum against Bitcoin. Meanwhile, data from Token Terminal reveals that Ethereum's staking ratio reached an all-time high of 32.33% on April 21. This increase in staking has reduced the liquid supply of Ether, even as it underperformed Bitcoin by approximately 5.5% over the past week.

data.title: An account with an 81% win rate purchased $40,000; Trump will continue to block the Strait of Hormuz until April 30.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer's monitoring, in the Polymarket prediction event "Trump will continue to block the Strait of Hormuz before April 30," an account with an 81% win rate (@Pajamapants) purchased $40,000 worth of positions, with an average opening price of 68.5¢, indicating that Trump would not end the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz before April 30. The specific rule is: if President Trump, the US government, or the military publicly and officially announces the end of the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz on the specified date (11:59 PM Eastern Time), the market will turn "yes." Otherwise, the market will ultimately decide "no." The statement must clearly and explicitly state that the US has lifted, ended, or will lift or end the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz on the specified date, or use equivalent, clear language to explicitly state that the blockade has ended or will end on the specified date (e.g., a statement explicitly stating that US naval activity in the relevant area has ceased qualifies). Statements that merely describe actions inconsistent with the blockade (e.g., "Iran resumes shipping through the Strait of Hormuz") without explicitly stating the lifting of the blockade are insufficient. Informal announcements, statements from anonymous sources, or leaks are not eligible. Written public statements by Donald Trump (such as posts on his personal Truth Social account) will be eligible. Videos posted by him on social media will also qualify for a “for” resolution. The primary source of solutions for this market will be official statements from the U.S. government and/or its official representatives; however, credible consensus reports may also be used. Note: This market will be determined solely based on whether a qualified announcement is issued within the specified timeframe. Whether a blockade is effectively enforced, or whether sea traffic has resumed, will not be considered without a qualified announcement. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Market Predicts 68% Probability of Trump's Impeachment by 2028
data.description: According to Jin10, prediction market data indicates a 68% probability that U.S. President Donald Trump will face impeachment by January 2028. This likelihood has been rising steadily from approximately 30% in November of last year. The shift suggests increasing market expectations of potential political challenges for Trump in the future, although the final outcome remains uncertain.

data.title: Cardano Treasury Proposals for 2026 Submitted by Input Output Global
data.description: Input Output Global has submitted nine proposals for Cardano's treasury funding for 2026. According to NS3.AI, the total funding request is nearly half of what was requested last year. The proposals focus on the development of Leios, which is anticipated to reach the testnet phase by June and aims for a mainnet launch by the end of 2026.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Zhipu's GLM Coding Plan Covers OpenClaw, Hermes, and SillyTavern
data.description: Zhipu product lead Zixuan Li announced that OpenClaw, Hermes, and SillyTavern are explicitly included in the support scope of the GLM Coding Plan. According to NS3.AI, Li emphasized that other general-purpose tools will be evaluated individually. He also cautioned users against sharing accounts or using subscriptions as APIs.

data.title: Zelensky stated that Ukraine is ready to hold talks with Russia in any form at any time.
data.description: Zelensky stated that Ukraine is ready to hold talks with Russia in any form at any time.

data.title: Binance launches Alpha page, showcasing Alpha Points and historical return data.
data.description: Binance has announced the launch of its Alpha page, showcasing the Alpha points system, historical Alpha event rewards, and progress data on related tokens' listing on Binance. This page will serve as a centralized entry point for Alpha-related information, allowing users to view historical performance and participation data.

data.title: Mexican Government Unaware of U.S. Involvement in Drug Lab Operation
data.description: The Mexican government was reportedly unaware of the participation of two U.S. officials in an operation aimed at dismantling a drug lab in northern Mexico. Bloomberg posted on X that the operation, which took place recently, involved collaboration between Mexican authorities and U.S. officials. However, the Mexican government was not informed about the involvement of the U.S. personnel in the operation. This incident highlights ongoing challenges in cross-border cooperation in combating drug-related activities. The lack of communication between the two governments raises concerns about the coordination and transparency of joint operations in the region.

data.title: Iran's Hormuz Strait Management Plan Includes Rial-Based Transit Fees
data.description: Iran's National Security Committee has outlined key provisions for managing the Hormuz Strait. According to Odaily, a member of the committee stated that one of the main clauses involves collecting transit fees in Iranian currency, the rial.

data.title: WLFI founder: Justin Sun's lawsuit against WLFI is baseless.
data.description: Zach Witkoff, founder and CEO of WLFI, stated that Justin Sun's recent lawsuit against World Liberty Financial is an attempt to divert attention from his own misconduct, and that his allegations are "completely unfounded." The company will seek to dismiss the case as soon as possible. He also stated that Sun's actions have prompted World Liberty to take measures to protect its own and its users' interests, and that the company will continue to take necessary actions to maintain community safety.

data.title: Projects such as OpenClaw and Hermes have been explicitly included in the GLM Coding Plan's support scope.
data.description: Zixuan Li, product manager at Zhipu, stated on the X platform that OpenClaw, Hermes, and SillyTavern are now explicitly marked as projects supported by the GLM Coding Plan. Other general-purpose tools will be analyzed on a case-by-case basis. Furthermore, Zixuan Li reminded users not to share accounts or use subscriptions as APIs. If users still encounter error 1313 while complying with the rules, they can contact the official support team for feedback.
